Title: Most weight lifted by rear lateral raises in one minute (male)
RecordText:
The most weight lifted by rear lateral raises in one minute is 1,845 kg (4,067.53 lb) and was achieved by Eamonn Keane (Ireland) at Louisburgh Gym in Louisburgh, Ireland, on 16 October 2013.
Eamonn used two 22.5 kg dumbbells and managed at total of 41 lifts within the one minute timeframe.

Title: Most stage flights performed
RecordText:
Performing a stunt known in Japanese as 'chunori', kabuki actor Ichikawa Ennosuke (b.9 Dec 1939) of Tokyo, Japan, has flown across the stage and audience for 5,000 performances since April 1968.
His 5,000th flight was achieved on 19 April 2000 at 3.08pm at the Shimbashi Ebujo Theatre in Tokyo, performing in Shin Sankokushi. Ennosuke plays a hero who dies and flies to heaven with his lover in his arms.

'Chunori', performed using pulleys and a safety belt was developed in Edo (today's Tokyo) in the 17th century and remained a popular stage tick for nearly three centuries.

Ennosuke revived the stunt at the National Theatre in Tokyo for the play Yoshitsune and the Thousand Cherry Trees and continued the 'chunori' in numerous guises and drama throughout the next 32 years.

He has also performed the stunt internationally, including performances in Berlin, Germany in 1985, Paris, France in 1987 and New York, USA in 1989.

Variations of the 'chunori' include strolling through the air by parasol and riding double on a horse.

Title: Largest marine protected area (IUCN definition)
RecordText:
Based on the IUCN’s assessment criteria for protected areas, the largest marine protected area (MPA), as of September 2018, is Papahānaumokuākea Marine National Monument around the north-western end of the Hawai’ian archipelago, USA. Originally inaugurated in June 2006 with an area of 360,000 square kilometres (140,000 square miles), on 26 August 2016, the-then US President Barack Obama announced an expansion that more than quadrupled the size of the reserve; it now covers a total area of 1,508,870 square kilometres (582,578 square miles). The reserve prevents any commercial fishing or mining activities and helps to protect more than 7,000 species.


To be officially recognized and endorsed as a "marine protected area”, an MPA must meet a number of strict standards agreed by the global community and laid out by the IUCN’s World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA) – including but not limited to:

- Conservation focused with nature as the priority

- Defined goals and objectives which reflect these conservation values

- Suitable size, location and design that will enable conservation of values

- Defined and readily understood boundary

- Management plan or equivalent, which address the needs for the conservation of the site’s major values and achievement of its social and economic goals and objectives

- Resources and capacity to implement

It must be noted that other marine areas have been designated that occupy a larger area than Papahānaumokuākea, including the Ross Sea Region Marine Protected Area (2.09 million square kilometres) in Antarctica and Marae Moana (1.99 million square kilometres) in the Cook Islands. However, based on current compliance with the principles and guidelines of the WCPA – and variable levels of conservation management and permitted usage within these reserves – the IUCN asserts that Papahānaumokuākea Marine National Monument is the most qualified to the title of largest MPA as of Sep 2018.

Title: Longest hair for a domestic cattle breed
RecordText: The breed of domestic cattle Bos taurus with the longest hair is the Highland cattle. Originating in Scotland but subsequently exported worldwide, this famously hairy breed has an average hair length of 35 cm. This length is taken from the length of the Dossan (fringe) and also the length of hair in the ears. Worth noting is that the coarse hair of the long-haired yak has a minimum hair length of 20 cm, but this domestic breed belongs to a second, separate species of cattle, Bos grunniens.

Title: Highest score on Klax (Atari 2600)
RecordText:
The highest score for Klax is 89,090, achieved by Nathan Page (UK), on the Atari 2600 version of the game, on 11 June 2004.

Twin Galaxies' referee Kelly R. Flewin is ranked second with 55,765.

Title: Most goals conceded by a football (soccer) team in a FIFA World Cup
RecordText: South Korea crashed out of the 1954 FIFA World Cup in Switzerland having conceded 16 goals in just two games. They lost 9–0 to Hungary and 7–0 to Hungary and finished bottom of Group 2.

Title: Most home runs consecutively to start a Major League Baseball match by a team
RecordText:
The Major League Baseball (MLB) record for most consecutive home runs hit at the beginning of a game is three, a feat accomplished eight times, most recently by the New York Mets batters Brandon Nimmo (USA), Francisco Lindor (Puerto Rico), and Jeff McNeil (USA) to begin an 8–0 victory over the Washington Nationals in Game 2 of a doubleheader, in Flushing, New York, on 4 October 2022.


The seven other teams to accomplish the feat are the Minnesota Twins on 9 June 2022; Arizona Diamondbacks on 10 June 2019 and 21 July 2017; Baltimore Orioles on 10 May 2012; Milwaukee Brewers on 9 September 2007; Atlanta Braves on 28 May 2003; and San Diego Padres on 13 April 1987.

Title: First row across the Atlantic west to east from Canada by a team of four
RecordText: The first Ocean Four to row the Atlantic West to East from Canada was the crew of Naturally Best, George Rock, Robert Munslow, Nigel Morris, Steve Dawson (all UK) between 31 May - 10 July 2005, who achieved this in the fastest time recorded for that route: 39 days 22 hr 10 min 30 sec.

Title: Most people playing Bunco simultaneously
RecordText:
The most people playing bunco simultaneously is 1,599, achieved by Addicted 2 Cuffs (USA), in Tulsa, Oklahoma, USA, on 24 February 2016.


Addicted 2 Cuffs is a group that comes together in appreciation of Rustic Cuff, a brand of jewellery in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The attempt took place at the Renaissance Tulsa Hotel and Convention Center.

Title: First global MMO convention
RecordText: The inaugural Ultima Online World Faire was held in Austin, Texas, USA, on 10–11 November 2000. The event, at Origin Systems’ HQ, saw some 800 players enjoy more than 50 panels and workshops, as well as tournaments, demos and merchandise stalls. The World Faire in Austin was also the first time an MMO expansion was unveiled at a convention.

Title: Worst cyclone disaster - damage toll
RecordText: According to reinsurers Swiss Re, Hurricane Katrina, which devastated the coast of Louisiana, USA on 29 August 2005, caused damage expected to reach a total of $156 billion (£90 billion). Katrina was the first category-5 hurricane of the 2005 hurricane season.

Title: Largest prehistoric insect
RecordText:
The largest prehistoric insect was the dragonfly Meganeura monyi, which lived about 280 million years ago. Fossil remains (i.e. impressions of wings) discovered at Commentry, France, indicate a wing expanse of up to 70 cm. (27.5 in).
This compares to the largest dragonfly living today (Megaloprepus caeruleata) from Central and South America which has been measured up to 120 mm (4.72 in) in length with a wingspan of up to 191 mm (7.52 in).

Title: First videogame to feature real NFL players and teams
RecordText: NFL Football, released for the NES in 1989, was intended to represent the 1988/89 NFL season, and used teams and play formations accurate to the era.

Title: Thickest ice
RecordText:
The thickest ice in the world forms part of the Antarctic Ice Sheet where it sits over a region known as the Astrolabe Subglacial Basin to the south of the Adélie Coast. Here, the ice sheet has been measured to be 4,897 metres (16,066 feet) thick. The basin is named after Astrolabe, the flagship of the French Antarctic Expedition (1837–40).


Ice thickness is often measured using ice-penetrating radar, and many areas of Antarctica remain unsurveyed, which means that thicker ice could exist but has yet to be measured.

Previously, the greatest recorded thickness of ice was 4,780 metres (15,682 feet), as measured by radio echo soundings from a US Antarctic research aircraft at 69°56'17 in Wilkes Land, eastern Antarctica.

The research that confirmed this as the thickest ice recorded to date was published in the journal The Cryosphere on 28 February 2013.

Title: Most appearances on a videogame cover by a soccer player (multiple franchises)
RecordText:
The most appearances on a videogame cover by a soccer player across multiple franchises is 12, and was achieved by Lionel Messi (Argentina), on 30 September 2021.


As of the release of eFootball 2022 in September 2021, Argentina's Lionel Messi has featured on 12 soccer videogame covers across the FIFA and eFootball (formally Pro Evolution Soccer, aka PES) franchises. Messi's full list of covers include: PES 2009, PES 2010, PES 2011, FIFA Street, FIFA 13, FIFA 14, FIFA 15, FIFA 16; PES 2017; PES 2018 Legendary Edition, both as part of a Barcelona line-up; and, most recently, eFootball 2020 and 2022.

Title: Largest display of mandalas
RecordText:
The largest display of mandalas is 11,786, and was achieved by Randeep Singh Nandal (India) in Rohtak, Haryana, India, on 31 March 2025.


Randeep broke his own record in honor of the 2025 Met Gala.

Title: Fastest marathon dressed as a lumberjack (male)
RecordText:
The fastest marathon dressed as a lumberjack (male) is 3 hr 39 min 12 sec, and was achieved by Kirk Millikan (USA), at the TCS London Marathon, in London, UK, on 23 April 2023.


This record was achieved at the 2023 TCS London Marathon.

Title: Largest parade of horse-drawn carriages
RecordText: The largest parade of horse-drawn carriages was achieved by 265 carriages at Tipperary Racecourse, Limerick, Ireland, on 3 August 2008.

Title: Most damage dealt by a single spell in Hearthstone
RecordText: On 20 September 2015, Hearthstone players Plank and KaptVoorvel set up a match between Priest and Paladin with the goal of dealing the most damage possible in a single turn. With seven copies of Prophet Velen on the Paladin side of the field, a spell dealing 1,343,228,898 damage was then doubled seven times to become 171,933,298,944 damage – the most ever dealt in a single Hearthstone turn.

Title: Longest standing points of interest (POIs) in Fortnite Battle Royale
RecordText: When Epic Game's Fortnite Battle Royale began on 26 September 2017, it contained just 10 named POIs – Greasy Grove, Pleasant Park, Retail Row, Anarchy Acres, Fatal Fields, Lonely Lodge, Flush Factory, Loot Lake, Moisty Mire and Wailing Woods. As of 13 March 2019, during Fortnite Season 8, only four of those POIs remain – Pleasant Park, Retail Row, Fatal Field and Lonely Lodge. Each had stood for 1 year and 169 days as the longest-lasting POIs in Fortnite.

Title: Fastest marathon by a linked team (female)
RecordText:
The fastest marathon by a linked team (female) is 3 hr 27 min 58 sec and was achieved by Team MitoCanada (Canada) at the Calgary Marathon in Calgary, Alberta, Canada, on 28 May 2017.


Team MitoCanada is a platform for raising awareness of Mitochondrial disease through sport. Team MitoCanada is made up of Mekita Aznar, Allison Blackmore, Shannyn Clancy, Dawn Ladds, Megan MacDonald, Jody Maull, Debbie Reed, Deb Russell, Louise Taylor, and Adriana Wild.

Title: Oldest wheelchair tennis player ranked world No.1
RecordText:
The oldest male or female world No.1, across all disciplines of professional tennis, is quad doubles legend David Wagner (USA, b. 4 March 1974), who was 44 years 273 days old at the conclusion of the Wheelchair Tennis Masters in Orlando, Florida, USA, on 2 December 2018. The Californian ace, who was paralyzed from the chest downwards at the age of 21 after an accident at Redondo Beach, has claimed 22 quad doubles and six quad singles Grand Slam titles (2007–22), and he took gold at three successive Paralympic Games in quad doubles: Athens 2004, Beijing 2008 and London 2012.


India’s Rohan Bopanna (b. 4 March 1980), who shares a birthday with Wagner, was 43 years 326 days old when he was confirmed as the ATP’s men’s doubles No.1 at the Australian Open on 24 January 2024.

The WTA record belongs to another doubles player, Lisa Raymond (USA, b. 10 August 1973), who was 39 years 30 days old on 9 September 2012.

Title: Most people angle diving (skydiving) simultaneously
RecordText:
The most people angle diving (skydiving) simultaneously is 25 and was achieved by World Peace Movement, as verified in Giza Plateau, Egypt, on 1 November 2024.


The team of 25, consisting of 12 different nationalities, exited the plane whilst hovering over the Pyramids of Giza in Egypt.

Title: Most people in a static equilibrium chair exercise
RecordText:
The most people in a static equilibrium chair exercise is 150, and was achieved by StudentLife Academy (Cyprus), in Nicosia, Cyprus, on 20 September 2019.


This record was broken by the students of StudentLife Academy and was used as a team building exercise on the first day of their programme.

Title: Oldest ice body
RecordText:
In September 1991, the mummy of a Neolithic man was discovered by two hikers in a melting glacier in the Tyrolean Alps on the Italian-Austrian border, 5,300 years after he had died. Analysis of 'Ötzi' and his belongings have given a new insight into the technology and culture of central Europe some 5,000 years ago.
Items found on and around the body included a bead with rawhide strings, a half-built bow, a copper axe and a cape made from grass. “Ötzi” was named after the region of the Alps where he had been found. In 2001, scientists discovered that “Ötzi” had blood from four different people on his clothes and an arrowhead in his shoulderblad. Latest research suggests he may have been infertile, supporting theories that “Ötzi” was an outcast from his tribe.

Title: Fastest time to sort 10,000 banknotes into note bundles (team of 50)
RecordText:
The fastest time to sort 10,000 banknotes into note bundles (team of 50) is 8 min 2.98 sec and was achieved by The Kita Osaka Shinkin Bank (Japan) in Ibaraki, Osaka, Japan, on 1 March 2025.


The Kita Osaka Shinkin Bank will celebrate its 100th anniversary in April 2025, and to mark the occasion, its staff attempted a Guinness World Records title.

Title: First person to win an Oscar award posthumously
RecordText: The first posthumous Oscar was awarded at the 1939 Academy Awards on 29 February 1940 to Sidney Howard for his screenplay of Gone With The Wind (USA 1939). He had been killed in a farm accident.

Title: Largest standing lantern
RecordHolder: Henan Dahe Cultural Development Co. Ltd
RecordText:
The largest standing lantern measures 45.03 m (147 ft 8.83 in) in length, 24.84 m (81 ft 5.95 in) in height, 19.70 (64 ft 7.59 in) m in width and was achieved by Luoyang Culture Tourism Investment Co., Ltd, Chengdu Daily Jin Guan Media Co., Ltd and Henan Dahe Cultural Development Co., Ltd (all China) in Luoyang, Henan, China, on 9 February 2024.


Luoyang is proud of its peonies which are the name card of the city. The organizer would like to build a magnificent standing lantern. not only to celebrate the traditional Chinese New Year, but also to add to the appeal of Luoyang as a tourist destination as well as effectively attracting people and promoting night consumption.

Title: Most Grammys won for Best Country Solo Performance
RecordText:
Chris Stapleton (USA) won a record-extending fifth award in the Best Country Solo Performance category at the 67th Grammy Awards on 2 February 2025, for “It Takes a Woman”, a track from his fifth studio album Higher (2023). Stapleton had previously earned the award for “Traveller” (2016), “Either Way” (2018), “You Should Probably Leave” (2022) and “White Horse” (2024). He saw off Beyoncé, Jelly Roll, Kacey Musgraves and Shaboozey to land the prize in 2025.


Carrie Underwood (2013 and 2015), Darius Rucker (2014), Maren Morris (2017), Kacey Musgraves (2019), Willie Nelson (2020 and 2023) and Vince Gill (2021) – country music’s most decorated Grammys recipient with 22 awards – have all won Best Country Solo Performance, first presented to Taylor Swift in 2012.

Title: Most badges pinned onto a person in one minute
RecordText:
The most badges pinned onto a person in one minute is 25 and was achieved by Robyn Richford and Kerry Boyne (both UK) on the set of Scrambled! (CITV), in Goudhurst, UK, on 4 March 2020.


The badges were all branded with the Scrambled! logo.

Title: Largest streamer string fight
Location: Ottawa,CE Center, a large event space. , Canada
RecordText:
The largest streamer string fight was fought by 629 participants and was organized by Funatorium Explorium at the CE Centre, in Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, on 20 February 2012.
The streamer string fight was the closing event at a three day celebration that coincided with Family Day, a new Canadian holiday.

Title: Largest baseball/softball pitching target
RecordText:
The largest baseball/softball pitching target consists of 100 panels, and was achieved by Fischer's (Japan) in Katsushika, Tokyo, Japan, on 23 June 2023.


The size of the 100 panel pitching target is 3.82 x 3.82 meters. The attempt details is uploaded on the Fischer's YouTube channel.

Title: Most three-point field goals converted in WNBA Finals (career)
RecordText: Diana Taurasi (USA) has converted 51 three-point field goals in her WNBA Finals career while playing for the Phoenix Mercury (USA) in 2007, 2009, 2014 and 2021.

Title: Oldest person to climb first 3 peaks (male)
RecordText:
The oldest person to climb first 3 peaks (male) was Carlos Soria (Spain), born on February 5th, 1939. This was achieved on May 18th, 2014, at the age of 75 years and 102 days.


This record title was verified by the mountaineering expert Eberhard Jurgalski.

Title: Fastest completion of Saints Row: Gat out of Hell
RecordText: Despite making some “major mistakes”, US gamer “redemption99” redeemed himself by polishing off the latest series addition Saints Row: Gat out of Hell in exactly 44 min 34 sec. He completed this devil-trumping feat on 19 February 2015, with his video uploaded to Twitch TV.

Title: Fastest time for a dog to weave 24 poles
RecordText:
The fastest time for a dog to weave between 24 poles is 5.88 sec and was achieved by Alma owned by Emilio Pedrazuela Cólliga (both Spain), on the set of 'Guinness World Records', Madrid, Spain, on 16 January 2009.
Alma was attempting the record as part of a 3-way challenge with each dog having two attempts. Her first attempt was 6.10 seconds. The best attempts by the other two dogs, Yuna and King, was 6.79 sec and 6.66 sec respectively.

Title: Most total passing yards in a Super Bowl game (both teams)
RecordText: The most combined total passing yards in a Super Bowl game is 874 and was achieved by the Philadelphia Eagles and New England Patriots (both USA) at U.S. Bank Stadium in Minneapolis, Minnesota, USA, on 4 February 2018, in a game the Eagles won 41–33.

Title: Fastest crossing of Luxembourg north to south on foot (male)
RecordText:
The fastest crossing of Luxembourg north to south on foot (male) is 13 hours, 30 minutes, 15 seconds and was achieved by Cieran English and Joseph Robson (both UK) on 26 October 2024.


Cieran and Joseph decided to break the record to raise money (£5000) for MS-UK and brain tumour research.

Title: Fastest 200 Km by tandem bicycle (WUCA) (male) (B2)
RecordText:
The fastest 200 kilometers by tandem bicycle (WUCA) (male) (B2) is 6 hours, 2 minutes, 38 seconds, and was achieved by Ricardo Paván (Mexico) and Raúl Villalba (Argentina) in Sebring, Florida, USA, on 12 March 2025.


This record was ratified by the World Ultra Cycling Association (WUCA).

Title: Most pubs visited in 24 hours by a team
RecordText:
The most pubs visited in 24 hours is 250 and was achieved by a team of 13 organised by Chris Solarz and Ivan Wanat (both USA) in New York, New York, USA, from 3-4 September 2011. The team members who took part were: Chris Solarz, Ivan Wanat, Matt Bockelman, Eric Bockelman, Brent Bockelman, Dalmau Reig, Wesley Erdelack, Michael Rasiej, Francis Laros, Steve McLaughlin, Rickey Gawley, Roger Wu and Bea Reig.


The attempt began at 8:44 am on Saturday and ended at 12:44 am on Sunday.

GWR does not require any alcohol to be consumed while attempting this record.

Title: Most costly movie costume
RecordText:
Hollywood studios are famously secretive when it comes to the accounting of production costs, which makes it difficult to know the identity of the holder for this title with certainty. Several famous costumes have been described as the "most expensive ever made", but the most credible candidate for the title is a fur-trimmed sequined showgirl ensemble created by legendary designer Edith Head for Ginger Rogers in Lady in the Dark (1944). Two versions of this dress were made for the production (a "hero costume" for static close-ups, and a lighter "multiple" for dancing) at a cost of $35,000, which is equivalent to $620,000 in 2023 when adjusted for inflation.


Although often simply called the "mink dress", the costume actually comprised three separate garments – a mink-fur bolero, tight-fitting bodysuit and an open-fronted overskirt. The bodysuit was decorated with thousands of sewn-on imitation jewels and sequins, hand-stitched into elaborate swirling patterns that extended down to the fingertips of the integral gloves. The overskirt's visible lining matched the bodysuit, while its exterior was entirely mink fur to match the bolero.

During fittings, it was found that the hero costume was too heavy and stiff for Rogers to dance in, so a lightweight multiple for dancing.

Title: Fastest trace the line relay (team of two)
RecordText:
The fastest time to trace the line relay (team of two) is 24.27 sec, and was achieved by Alison Engel and Anna Villanyi (both USA) at the Toronto Convention Center in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on 22 September 2019.


This attempt was done in promotion for Science of Guinness World Records.

Title: Youngest golfer to win an event on the Ladies European Tour
RecordText: Atthaya Thitikul (Thailand, b. 20 February 2003) won the Ladies European Thailand Championship on 6–9 July 2017 aged 14 years 139 days. She finished the event at Phoenix Gold Golf and Country Club in Pattaya, Thailand, on a five-under-par score of 283, two ahead of the field. As an amateur player, Thitikul was unable to claim the 45,000-euro ($51,322; £39,838) winner’s prize.

Title: Most participants in a bleep test
RecordText:
The most participants in a bleep test is 941 and was achieved by AFC Harrogate (UK) in Harrogate, UK, on 14 December 2017.


AFC Harrogate attempted this record as part of their graduation week.

Title: Largest indoor water park
RecordText:
The largest indoor water-based theme park is reported to be Tropical Islands, Krausnick, Germany, with a 66,000-m² (710,400-ft²) main hall – technically an arch-supported membrane structure – with 14,000 tons (30,864,716 lb) of steel and 107 m (351 ft) high, big enough to contain 8 soccer pitches, hold the Statue of Liberty standing upright and the Eiffel Tower on its side. Opened in 2004 between Dresden and Germany, Tropical Islands was originally an airship hangar, and today is fitted out with a mangrove jungle, 50,000-plant rainforest, Tropical Sea, Bali Lagoon, spa, Angkor Wat-style sauna, themed accommodation venues and evening entertainment. It's even possible to go for a balloon ride inside the dome, while a new foil roof also lets in ultraviolet light, so that guests can get a tan. Tropical Islands can accommodate up to 6,000 visitors per day.
The airship hangar was constructed in 1999-2000 on the site of the former military Brand-Briesen military airfield by the CargoLifter company, and was intended for the production of the CL160 airship, a 550,000-m³ (19,423,066-ft³) vessel designed to carry a 160-tonne (352,739.62-lb) cargo. Built by a subsidiary of Siemens, SIAT, the dome roof has a surface area of 70,000 m² (753,470 ft²) and contains over 5 million m³ (176,573,333 ft³) of air. After bankruptcy in June 2002, the site was sold in 2003 to the owners of Tropical Islands.

Title: First row on the Southern Ocean
RecordText: The first row of the Southern Ocean is by captain Fiann Paul (Iceland), first mate Colin O’Brady, Andrew Towne, John Petersen (all USA), Jamie Douglas Hamilton (UK) and Cameron Bellamy (South Africa), who rowed 604 miles (525 nautical miles; 972 km) from Cape Horn, Chile, to Charles Point, mainland Antarctic Peninsula, in 12 days 1 hr 45 min between 13 and 25 December 2019 on board Ohana.

Title: Most entries in a handheld point n' click game series
RecordText:
With five commercially released games for Nintendo DS and three Japan-only Game Boy Advance titles, the Ace Attorney franchise boasts more entries than any other handheld point n click adventure game series. However, the hugely successful Professor Layton series appears to be a likely contender for this record in the near future, with four releases over the last three years.
Featured in Guinness World Records Gamer''s Edition 2010

Title: Most bottle caps removed with a helicopter in 3 minutes
RecordText:
The most bottle caps removed with a helicopter in 3 minutes is 9 and was achieved by Zhao Yang (China) on the set of Lo Show dei Record in Wuxi, Jiangsu, China, on 1 December 2024.


The challenger Zhao Yang is a helicopter pilot since 2011. He practiced years of piloting helicopter to do the accurate jobs. Before the attempt, they prepared 15 bottles of beer, Zhao finished removing all the caps within 2.06 seconds. after examining the bottles, 9 of them were intact and counted towards the record.

Title: Longest span inverted fink truss bridge
RecordHolder: Hangzhou Urban & Rural Construction Design Institute Co., Ltd.
RecordText: The longest span inverted fink truss bridge is 150.00 m (492 ft 1.51 in) and was achieved by Hangzhou Grand Canal Comprehensive Conservation Development and Construction Group Co., Ltd., Hangzhou Urban & Rural Construction Design Institute Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Hydro Construction Group Co., Ltd. (all China), in Hangzhou, Zhejiang, China, on 25 October 2023.

Title: Oldest president ever
RecordText:
The oldest president on record is Paul Biya (b. 13 February 1933), the fifth Prime Minister of Cameroon from 1975 to 1982 and second President of Cameroon from 1982 to the present. He was aged 92 years 5 days as of 18 February 2025, also making him the world's oldest serving head of state.


Biya was appointed Cameroon's Prime Minister in 1975 and was sworn in as President on 6 November 1982. He was re-elected - as the sole candidate - in 1984 and 1988, and despite the introduction of multi-party politics in the 1990s, his Cameroonian People's Democratic Movement (RDPC) has, according to the BBC, "won landslide majorities in every parliamentary election since 1992".

Recent rumours of his death were quashed when Biya was seen in public in October 2024.

Title: Most break repeater shells in a firework
RecordText:
On Friday, 7 July 2000, an eight-break repeater shell was successfully fired into the air at Goodwood Racecourse near Chichester, UK, by Kimbolton Fireworks Limited.


The black powder charge or lift is ignited by the hand fuse which explodes beneath the multi break shell contained within the mortar tube. This violent explosion forces the multi break shell out of the tube into the sky to a height of about 122m 400ft . At the bottom of the first shell is a two second delay which is lit when the lifting charge explodes. Therefore two seconds into its flight the first shell will burst blowing a mass of stars into the sky. This is turn lights the next shell above it. Again a two second delay is lit which then explodes the second shell.

This is repeated until the last shell is lit. The whole multibreak shell or cylinder shell remains intact as each shell contained inside explodes. The difficulty with these shells is to ensure a sufficient gap between each break and that all the shells are lit. The limit to these shells is the old adage, what goes up must come down. The most number of breaks is limited to how high the shell goes in the first place and how many breaks it can let off before it comes back down to the ground. It is therefore very difficult to make a shell which can do any more than 8 breaks.

Title: Largest soap bubble net
RecordText:
The largest soap bubble net is 28.635 square metres, and was achieved by Chloe Ferguson and Stuart Ferguson (both Guernsey) in St Sampsons, Guernsey, on 7 May 2022.


Chloe has been making her own bubble equipment for a while and upon seeing the previous record, she knew she would be able to achieve it...so she did!

Planning didn't take long as she just made sure she was following the set guidelines. She wanted the net to be the length of her open plan living/dining room. Altogether, the process took around 36 hours to complete over the space of several months. She explains that the net making process was relatively easy as she already knew how to make them. The issue was finding poles that could hold the sheer weight and to actually be tall enough to lift the net off the ground.

On the day of the day attempt, the weather was quite windy which made it harder to show that the bubble net was functional. It took a few attempts and in hindsight, she would choose different poles and create a platform so she was higher off the ground. Chloe had moral support from her husband throughout the process. She also had the help of Marquis Surveys who she says were lovely and offered surveying which is difficult to find coming from such a small island.

When asked about what the future holds, Chloe says "There are a lot of bubble records that I would love to attempt in the future! I'm also very interested in the longest garland wand."

"[Having a GWR title] would mean the WORLD to me! To achieve an accolade for what I love doing and knowing what I created is the biggest in the world would be a truly amazing feat."

Title: Shortest time between goals - football
RecordText:
Wycombe Wanderers scored two goals in nine seconds against Peterborough United at Adams Park, Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, UK on September 23, 2000.
Wycombe scored their first goal in the 46th minute from a free kick awarded by referee John Brandwood who blew the whistle for the end of the first half immediately after the goal had been scored. The second goal was scored by striker Jermaine McSporran at the start of the second half with only eight seconds having elapsed.

Signed photo/video consent form received Jan 2002

Title: Longest orbital flight by a reusable spacecraft
RecordText:
The longest orbital flight by a reusable spacecraft is 2 years 50 days, achieved by the Boeing X-37B autonomous spaceplane between 2017 and 2019. The X-37B's record-setting mission began on 7 September 2017, when it was launched atop a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station. It came back down on 27 October 2019, touching down on the old Space Shuttle landing runway at the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, USA.


The X-47B is a 8.91-m-long (29-ft 3-in) spaceplane, similar in design to the Space Shuttle but only around a quarter of the size. It has a payload bay around the size of a pickup-truck bed and a solar array that is deployed once it reaches orbit. The X-37B's official name is the "Orbital Test Vehicle", or "OTV", and this mission (OTV-5) was carried out to test experimental satellite payloads and technologies. Much about the X-37B's uses and capabilities is shrouded in secrecy, leading to a great deal of speculation about its actual purpose. Amateur observers noticed that it raised and lowered its orbit during its mission, but the reasons for these manoeuvres are not clear. The pentagon has categorically denied that the X-37B is a weapon, or intended to carry weapons.

Previous records in this category were set by the X-37 in between 2010, 2015 and 2017. Before the X-37B, the record was held by Space Shuttle mission STS-80, which lasted for 17 days 15 hours 53 minutes between 19 November and 7 December 1996.

Title: Largest quadrille dance - multiple locations
Location: 51 cities in 8 countries - Slovenia, Croatia, Serbia, Former Republic of Macedonia, Hungary, Czech Republic, Bosnia and Hercegovina, Slovakia, Slovenia
RecordText: The greatest number of people performing a quadrille dance simultaneously at different venues is 33,202 and was coordinated by the Slovene Dance Federation (Slovenia) at the European Quadrille Dance Festival in 51 cities in 8 countries on 20 May 2011.

Title: ISSF shooting, mixed 10 m air rifle prone (SH1)
RecordHolder: Jonas Jakobsson, Natascha Hiltrop, Cedric Fevre
RecordText:
Jonas Jakobsson (Sweden) and Natascha Hiltrop (Germany) both amassed 600 points in the mixed (male and female) SH1 classification 10 metres air rifle (prone position), Jakobsson in Jarvenpaa, Finland, on 1 August 1995 and Hiltrop in the qualifying round at the London 2012 Paralympic Games on 1 September. The final world and Paralympic record stands at 706.7 points and was set by London 2012 gold medallist Cedric Fevre (France) on 1 September.
The Paralympic mixed 10 metres air rifle prone record (excluding finals) is 600 points and was set by Enayatollah Bokharaei (Iran) in Atlanta, USA, on 21 August 1996 and equalled by both joint world record-holder Natascha Hiltrop (see record text for details) and Jae Yong Sim (South Korea) in the qualifying round at the London 2012 Paralympic Games on 1 September.

Title: Most marble slabs broken with nunchaku strikes in one minute
RecordText:
The most marble slabs broken with nunchaku strikes in one minute is 61, and was achieved by Muhammad Rashid (Pakistan) in Karachi, Sind, Pakistan, on 1 June 2025.


Muhammad slammed his nunchaku through 61 marble slabs with power and speed.

Title: Most nationalities in a trading lesson
CurrentRecord: Most Nationalities in a trading lesson people
Location: Dubai, United Arab Emirates, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
RecordText:
The most nationalities in a trading lesson is 54 and was achieved by Delta Training Academy, Carlton Fx and Miles Capital in association with Futurax Marketing Agency (all UAE) in Dubai, UAE, on 19 October 2024.


No duplicate nationalities were present during the attempt

Title: Most skips whilst jumping long rope simultaneously in one minute
Location: Beijing, Beijing, China Mainland, Beijing, China Mainland
RecordText:
The most skips whilst jumping long rope simultaneously is 300 and was achieved by Li Kehao, Liu Yuanxin and Wang Ruiyang (all China) from Shandong Zhuoyue Skipping Team on the set of CCTV Impossible Challenge in Beijing, China, on 5 August 2020.


The attempt was broadcast on CCTV App.

Title: Most varieties of beer commercially available
RecordText: The most varieties of beer commercially available was 2,004 at Delíríum Café, Brussels, Belgium when counted on 9 January 2004.

Title: Most times to host the US Amateur Golf Championship by a course
RecordText: Two golf courses have hosted the US Amateur Golf Championship on a total of six occasions, The Country Club, in Brookline, Massachusetts, USA, between 1910 and 1982; and Merion Golf Club, Ardmore, Pennsylvania, USA, between 1916 and 2005.

Title: Largest LEGO® brick Horizon: Zero Dawn custom scale model
RecordText: After creating LEGO® masterpieces from Titanfall and Halo, Marius Herrmann put together Horizon Zero Dawn’s fearsome Thunderjaw, which measured 42 cm x 30 cm x 65 cm (16.5 in x 11.8 in x 25.6 in). Guerrilla Games’s studio art director called it “fantastic”.

Title: Youngest Titanic survivor
RecordText:
Millvina Dean (UK, b. 2 February 1912) was just 72 days old when she travelled third class on the Titanic cruiseliner with her parents and 18-month-old brother. She, along with her mother and brother, all survived when the "unsinkable" ship hit an iceberg on 14 April 1912 and began to sink, but her father, Bert, was among the 1,517 passengers who perished in the infamous tragedy.


Of the 706 passengers who travelled in third class aboard the Titanic, only 178 survived - 75 of the 462 men (16.23%) 76 of the 165 women (46.06%) and 27 of the 79 children (34.17%).

In total there were 109 children on board the ship, 57 of which were saved (52.29%).

Title: Longest journey by fire engine
RecordText:
The longest journey by a fire engine is 50,957 km (31,663 miles) long and was achieved by Stephen Moore (UK), who started his journey from Greenwich, UK, on 18 July 2010 and finished it at the same place, on 10 April 2011.


Stephen was supported throughout his journey by his crew: Chris Moore, Ross Shepley-Smith, George Troughton, Ben Battye, Ben Haslett, James Morrow, David Orders, Ailsa Fereday, Paul Barham, James Stevens, Michelle Mahoney, Lyndsey Baker, Seve Poderoso, Amy Harpin, Louise Mahoney, Fuad Sulejmanovic, Kate Bartishel, Chris Hallward, Rob Poderoso, Valerie Keith, Gemma Rowlan, Steve Shoppman, Matt Haynes and Mehul Patel (all UK), who took turns to accompany him.

The fire engine was a Mercedes 1124 AF named Martha, which was designed for short and quick journeys.

Title: Fastest time to limbo skate under descending poles over 50 m
RecordText:
The fastest time to limbo skate under descending poles over 50 m is 7.46 seconds and was achieved by Shrishti Dharmendra Sharma (India), in Umbred, Maharashtra, India on 28 February 2025.


Shrishti first became a Guinness World Records title holder in 2023 when she attempted this title on the set of Lo Show Dei Record, in Monza, Italy. Today, she has surpassed her previous record of 9.59 secs by just a mere 2.13 secs!

Limbo skating is basically just what it sounds like! It's a sport, (usually done on roller skates) in which skaters glide under extremely low horizontal bars without touching them. Think the limbo dance, but on wheels."

Shrishti trained diligently for this record attempt; she hopes this achievement helps inspire and convinces girls worldwide that they can achieve anything boys can.

Title: Most consecutive birdies recorded by an individual at the US Open golf tournament
RecordText: During the first round of the 2017 US Open at Erin Hills, Wisconsin, USA, Adam Hadwin (Canada) made birdies at six consecutive holes: the 18th and holes 1–5. He matched the feat of George Burns in 1982 and Andy Dillard (both USA) in 1992, both achieved at Pebble Beach Golf Links in California, USA.

Title: Highest energy gamma rays from a gamma ray burst
RecordText: On 3 May 2013, NASA announced its Fermi Gamma-Ray Space Telescope had detected a gamma ray burst in a galaxy some 3.6 billion light years away. Gamma-ray bursts are the most powerful events in the Universe, and are believed to be the birth cries of black holes, as supermassive stars exhaust their fuel and collapse to a singularity. This explosion, known as GRB 130427A, emitted gamma radiation with an energy of at least 94 billion electron volts, or some 35 billion times the energy of normal visible light.

Title: Largest methane sea
RecordText: Kraken Mare, on Titan, is a body of liquid methane 1,170 km (727 miles) across and with an area of around 400,000 km² (154,000 sq mi) – making it slightly larger than Earth’s Caspian Sea. In July 2009, Cassini imaged the glint of reflected sunlight off Kraken Mare.

Title: Fastest run 100 metres (T42, female)
RecordText: The fastest run 100 metres (T42, female) is 14.64 seconds, achieved by Karisma Evi Tiarani (Indonesia) in Nottwil, Switzerland, on 27 May 2022.

Title: Most views for a YouTube channel dedicated to a single videogame
RecordText: Launched on 14 July 2012, “DanTDM” (formerly known as “TheDiamondMinecart”) by Daniel Middleton (UK) had 9,810,971,245 views as of 24 April 2017. The channel focuses on Minecraft.

Title: Fastest completion of Peach's Secret Slide Race on Super Mario 64 (Nintendo, 1996)
RecordText:
The fastest completion of Peach's Secret Slide on Super Mario 64 (Nintendo, 1996) is 12.40 seconds, achieved by Karl Parris (UK), in Lymington, Hampshire, UK, on 10 February 2012.
This record was broken on the Guinness World Record Challengers site (Challengers' tag: paradoxkarl): http://challengers.guinnessworldrecords.com/challenges/62-fastest-completion-of-peachs-secret-slide-in-super-mario-64-nintendo-64

Title: Most three-point field goal attempts missed by an individual in an NBA game
RecordText: The most three-point field goal attempts missed by an individual in an NBA game is 16 first set by Damon Stoudamire (5-for-21) of the Portland Trail Blazers against the Golden State Warriors on 15 April 2005, and equaled three times by James Harden of the Houston Rockets. Harden was 2-for-18 against the New Orleans Pelicans on 26 October 2019; 1-for-17 against the Orlando Magic on 13 January 2019; and 7-for-23 against the Sacramento Kings on 30 March 2019. Harden’s 1-for-17 performance from three-point range is also the record for worst three-point field goal percentage in a game in NBA history (minimum 15 attempts) at 5.9 percent.

Title: Largest sludge treatment plant
RecordHolder: The Arab Contractors (Osman Ahmed Osman & Co)
RecordText:
The largest sludge treatment plant is New Delta Irrigation Water Treatment Plant with a capacity of 670.01 Kg and achieved by Orascom Construction, The Arab Contractors (Osman Ahmed Osman & Co), Hassan Allam Construction, and Metito (All Egypt), in Dabaa, Egypt on 4 August 2023.


Delta Treatment Plant is also the record holders of the Largest water treatment plant and Largest water treatment facility.

Title: Highest free throw percentage WNBA season
RecordText: The highest free throw percentage in a WNBA single season (minimum 30 free throw attempts) is 100 percent by Becky Hammon (USA) who made all 35 of her free throw attempts while playing for the San Antonio Stars (USA) in 2014, and Veronica Burton (USA), who made all 32 of her attempts for the Dallas Wings (USA) in 2022.

Title: Most simultaneous games of backgammon
RecordText:
The most games of backgammon played simultaneously is 322, achieved by Albena Resort and Ouzo Plomari (both Bulgaria), in Albena Resort, Bulgaria, on 16 July 2016.


The 644 participants played for one hour.

Title: First row across the Arctic Ocean Open Waters north to south
RecordText:
The first recorded row across the Arctic Ocean Open Waters north to south is by Fiann Paul (Iceland), Alex Gregory, Danny Longman, Sam Vye (all UK), Carlo Facchino and Tyler Carnevale (both USA), who rowed 835 miles (1,344 km; 726 nautical miles) from Longyearbyen, Svalbard to Jan Mayen Island, Norway, in 13 days 17 hr 30 min between 8 and 21 August 2017 on board Polar Row.


The definition of "Rows on the Arctic Ocean Open Waters" applies only to pure rowing expeditions across major water basins above the Polar Circle from land to land, excluding any use of sail, paddling on kayaks or canoes, as well as rows around islands, within archipelagos and coastal rows, i.e. within vicinity of land and possibility to get ashore.

Title: Most Australian football goals scored from a handball pass in one minute from the boundary line (team of two)
Location: Rooty Hill, New South Wales, Australia
RecordText:
The most Australian football goals scored from a handball pass in one minute from the boundary line by a team of two is 18 and was achieved by Emma Swanson and Renee Tomkins (both Australia) on CBBC's Officially Amazing, in Rooty Hill, Australia, on 18 September 2017.


This attempt took place as part of filming for series seven of Officially Amazing.

Title: Spike driving - fastest
RecordText: In the World Championship Professional Spike Driving Competition held at the Golden Spike National Historic Site in Utah, USA, Dale C. Jones, 49, of Lehi, Utah, USA drove six 17.8cm 7in railroad spikes in a time of 26.4sec on 11 Aug 1984. He incurred no penalty points under the official rules.

Title: Smallest gap traversed
RecordText:
The smallest gap through which a human body has passed, and emerged alive, is 12.5 cm (5 in), when a high-powered conveyor system dragged plate welder Matthew Lowe (UK) into machinery at a factory in Barnsley, South Yorkshire, UK, in December 2008.
The 22-year-old Lowe emerged from the machine - which processed heavy steel beams ­ with a broken back, a ruptured stomach and bowel, a shattered pelvis, and fractured hips, ribs and right arm, despite passing through a gap no larger than a CD case.

Title: Most points scored in a NFL season
RecordText: La Dainian Tomlinson (USA), playing for the San Diego Chargers, scored the most points in an NFL single-season with 186 in 2006.

Title: Fastest time to solve a rotating puzzle cube while hula hooping
RecordText:
The fastest time to solve a rotating puzzle cube while hula hooping is 8.60 seconds, and was achieved by Lim Kai Yi (Malaysia) in Cheras, Malaysia, on 9 February 2023.


Lim Kai Yi has previously achieved over 30 different GWR titles including the fastest time to solve a 4 x 5 Klotski puzzle blindfolded and the fastest time to complete a Soma cube blindfolded. He is an avid speed solver and has won many competitions. His first win came in a state-level 3x3 competition.

Title: Largest online shop
RecordText: Amazon.com (USA), incorporated on 28 May 1996 and headquartered in Seattle, Washington, USA, reported net revenue of $177.866 billion for 2017, when it employed approximately 566,000 people. The online retailer was ranked 83rd in the 2017 Forbes' Global 2000 list of the world’s biggest and most powerful public companies, and its estimated market value reached $900 billion in July 2018.

Title: Longest duration surfing a wave - windsurfing
RecordText: Longest duration windsurfing a wave is 7 min 3 sec and was achieved by Camille Juban (France) at Chicama, Ascope, Peru, on 19 August 2013.

Title: Most strawberries dipped in chocolate in one minute
RecordText: The most strawberries dipped in chocolate in one minute is 59 and was achieved by Benjamin Wilkins (UK) in Oxfordshire, UK, on 29 October 2013.

Title: Longest garlic string
RecordHolder: Agricultural Cooperative of Production Elaboration - Trading of Garlic Vissas
RecordText:
The longest garlic string is 612.70 metres (2010.17 ft), achieved by Agricultural Cooperative of Production Elaboration - Trading of Garlic Vissas, and Evros Chamber of Commerce (both Greece), in Alexandroupoli, Evros, Greece, on 19 June 2018.


The event took place during the Alexandroupolis International fair "Alexpo 2018". Evros is a region known for its garlic production.

Title: Most MTV Video Music Award nominations for video of the year
RecordText:
The most MTV Video Music Award nominations for video of the year is 7, achieved by Beyoncé Knowles-Carter (USA) on 16 July 2018.


Beyoncé was nominated for the seventh time for her work on "APES**T" by The Carters. This was the second time Beyonce and husband Jay-Z have been nominated for video of the year as a duo -- the first being their song "Drunk in Love" in 2014.

Title: Fastest walk 50,000 metres (male)
RecordText: The fastest walk 50,000 metres (male) was 3 hr 35 min 27.2 sec by Yohann Diniz (France) in Reims, France, on 12 March 2011.

Title: Smallest recorded size of the Arctic ice cap
RecordText:
The Arctic ice cap is ice floating on the surface of the Arctic ocean. Its extension changes with the seasons, being the largest at the end of the winter and the smallest at the end of summer. The smallest size was registered on 17 September 2012, with an area of 3.39 million km² (1.31 million square miles).


According to some scientists, the decrease in the ice cap area is owing to the increase of the average Earth temperature (global warming). Since the recording of the Arctic ice cap began in 1979, the minimum size shrunk by about 2 million km² (0.77 million squares miles) – roughly equivalent to the area of Germany, France, UK, Spain and Italy put together.

Title: Most dice "pinch" picked up in 30 seconds (team of two)
RecordText:
The most dice “pinch” picked up in 30 seconds (team of two) is 29, and was achieved by Silvio Sabba and Luca Pincelli (both Italy), in Milan, Italy, on 12 December 2013.
This record was broken on the Guinness World Record Challengers site (Challengers' tag: silvio.sabba): http://challengers.guinnessworldrecords.com/challenges/290-most-dice-pinch-picked-up-in-30-seconds-team-of-two/attempts/4761

Title: Fastest any% completion of Splatoon
RecordText: US gamer “movefish" finished the 2015 WiiU game Splatoon – in "Any%" mode – in 51 minutes 19 seconds on 29 April 2017, as verified by Speedrun.com.

Title: Most videogame releases for a single intellectual property in one year
RecordText: In 2000, seven separate Star Trek licensed games were released, the highest number of game releases for an IP in a single year. The seven games were Star Trek: Voyager: Elite Force, Star Trek: ConQuest Online, Star Trek: Deep Space Nine: The Fallen, Star Trek: Starfleet Command II: Empires at War, Star Trek: New Worlds, Star Trek: Armada and Star Trek: Klingon Academy.

Title: Fastest marathon on stilts (male)
RecordText:
The record for the fastest marathon on stilts is 6 hr 50 min 02 sec and was achieved by George and Charley Phillips (both UK) at the 2012 Virgin London Marathon in London, UK, on 22 April 2012.


Brother and sister George and Charley have been walking on stilts since the age of 8.

Title: Oldest person to perform as a supporting artist on a TV show
RecordText:
The oldest person to perform as a supporting artist on a TV show is Jack Reynolds (UK b. 6 April 1912) aged 107 years 2 days, on the set of Hollyoaks (Lime Pictures), in Liverpool, UK, on 8 April 2019.


Jack's scene was broadcast on Friday 14th June on Channel 4. He appeared opposite regular Hollyoaks character Maxine Minniver, played by Nikki Sanderson. This is Jacks fourth Guinness World Records title.

Title: Referee in most Stanley Cup finals games
RecordText: Bill McCreary (Canada) has skated as the referee in 43 career Stanley Cup Finals games, a National Hockey League (NHL) record for referees. When McCreary and his whistle took the ice in Game 5 of the Stanley Cup Finals on 6 June 2010, he surpassed the previous mark of 42 set by Bill Chadwick (b. 10 October 1915, d. 24 October 29, USA). McCreary has refereed more than 1,600 NHL games since making his first start in the 1984-85 season. His first Stanley Cup Final-series assignment came during the 1994 Finals.

Title: Youngest driver to 100 Formula One podium finishes
RecordText:
The youngest driver to 100 Formula 1 podium finishes is Lewis Hamilton (UK, b.7 January 1985), who was aged 31 years 276 days when he finished third at the Japanese Grand Prix at the Suzuka Circuit in Suzuka, Mie Prefecture, Japan, on 9 October 2016.


Lewis Hamilton became only the third driver to reach 100 podiums after Alain Prost (in 1993) and Michael Schumacher (in 2002).

Prost was in his final season and 38 years old when he recorded his century of podiums and while Schumacher would go onto achieve a record 155 podiums he was already 33 years 2 months old when he picked up his 100th podium at the 2002 Brazilian Grand Prix.

Hamilton achieved the feat in 186 starts – five less than Prost – but Schumacher got there on just 163 starts.

Title: Most people in a team to row the Arctic Ocean Open Waters
RecordText:
The most people in a team to row the Arctic Ocean Open Waters is six, by Fiann Paul (Iceland), Alex Gregory, Danny Longman, Sam Vye (all UK), Carlo Facchino and Tyler Carnevale (both USA), who rowed 835 miles (1,344 km; 726 nautical miles) from Longyearbyen, Svalbard, to Jan Mayen Island, Norway. The row was completed in 13 days 17 hr 30 min between 8 and 21 August 2017 on board Polar Row.


The definition of "Rows on the Arctic Ocean Open Waters" applies only to pure rowing expeditions across major water basins above the Polar Circle from land to land, excluding any use of sail, paddling on kayaks or canoes, as well as rows around islands, within archipelagos and coastal rows, i.e., within the vicinity of land and with the possibility to get ashore.

Title: Most common type of space junk
RecordText:
The most common type of space junk is known as "fragmentation debris", or the remnants on on-orbit explosions and collisions (also known as "breakup events"). According to figures published by the NASA Orbital Debris Program Office in 2018, fragmentation debris represents 52.6% of all tracked objects in Earth orbit.


As of 31 Dec 2020, there have been 566 documented "on-orbit fragmentation events" (satellites or rocket bodies exploding in space). There are about 12 of these events every year. Most commonly, they are caused by the propulsion systems (explosions of left-over fuel) but intentional destruction (anti-satellite weapon tests) and unintentional collisions also play a role.

Title: First aerial photograph by kite
RecordText:
The first undisputed photos captured by a kite-borne camera, as recognized by the Kite Aerial Photography Worldwide Association in 1985, were snapped by photography pioneer Arthur Batut (France, 1846–1918) over Labruguière, Tarn, France, in May 1888. Titled "Le Chemin et le Ruisseau" ("The Track and the Brook"), the work is now housed (along with his equipment and many other of his photos) at the dedicated Espace Photographique Artur Batut museum in Labruguière.


Batut built a 2.5-m-tall (8-ft 2-in) , 1.75-m (5-ft 9-in) diamond-shaped kite with paper and a sturdy frame of Carolina poplar wood. The lightweight camera was made from wood, kraft paper and cork fitted with a 9 x 12-cm glass plate negative and a "Guillotine" shutter that was triggered by the releasing of elastic bands after a tinder-wick fuse has burned.

There is a claim by another photography pioneer and meteorologist, Edmund Archibald (UK), to have captured aerial photographs using a kite the previous year in 1887, employing a small explosive charge to activate the shutter, however his alleged photographs were never published, only limited details about the equipment, location and date were given (as well as conflicting dates of 1887 and July 1888 by Archibald) and no witnesses were present to attest to the experiments taking place, so evidence is severely lacking.

The first person to take an aerial photograph was Nadar, aka Gaspard-Félix Tournachon (France, 1820–1910), who in 1858 photographed the French village of Petit-Bicêtre (Petit-Clamart) from an air balloon tethered at an altitude of 80 m (262 ft). The oldest aerial photograph in existence today is a shot of Boston in Massachusetts, USA, taken from a balloon in 1860 by James Wallace Black (USA).

Title: Most critically acclaimed reboot of a shooter videogame
RecordText:
The Xbox One version of DOOM (2016) had a GameRankings score of 89.04%, from 24 reviews, as of 16 March 2017.


“A great and faithful encapsulation of its predecessors’ fast-paced shooting, trademark gore and infamous demons,” said gaming site DarkZero. The game's percentage put it ahead of fellow FPS reboots Return to Castle Wolfenstein (86.75%), Shadow Warrior (75.47%) and DOOM3 (87.63%). The latter, despite its name, is a reboot.

Title: Highest  wink shot in Tiddlywinks
RecordText:
The Tiddlywinks record for the highest wink shot is 11ft 5in (3.49m) and is tied by Adrian Jones, David Smith and Ed Wynn all of Cambridge University Tiddlywinks Club on October 21, 1989.
Note from Patrick Barrie: Each of the three people named individually beat the previous record, and so they are "joint holders". The reason they all independently achieved the same height is that it was the highest that we could raise the bar in the location.
